pages.title: About Peter
pages.meta_description: A former Merseyside County Councillor and leader of Sefton Council, Peter Dowd is the Labour MP for Bootle. He currently serves as Shadow Chief Secretary to the Treasury.
pages.text_excerpt: About Peter Peter Dowd Home About Gallery Contact Privacy Notice My work in the community My work in Parliament My views Home About Gallery Contact Peter Dowd Labour Member of Parliament for Bootle Privacy Notice My work in the community My work in Parliament My views About Peter I have been involved in our community for over 40 years in various public roles within the Council, Fire and Rescue Service, NHS and Education and Social Care Services. My roots are deep within our local communities and I intend them to remain that way. I believe that my experiences of working across various public bodies, the voluntary, charity and faith sectors, as well as links with trade unions and the business sector, have given me an insight into the pressures, challenges and issues affecting our people and our communities. My role is to be a champion and voice for our communities in Westminster. Peter Dowd Opportunities House 270 Marsh Lane Bootle L20 5BW 0151 933 8432 peter.dowd.mp@parliament.uk Info About Gallery Contact Privacy Policy Working for you My work in Bootle My work in Parliament My views Peter Dowd MP 270 Marsh Lane, Bootle, England, L20 5BW, United Kingdom 0151 933 8432 peter.dowd.mp@parliament.uk Hours

pages.title: Contact Peter
pages.meta_description: Get in touch with Peter Dowd MP. If you have an issue and live in Bootle constituency, Crosby, Waterloo, Seaforth, Litherland, Netherton, Orrell and Ford, Peter may be able to help.
pages.text_excerpt: Contact Peter Peter Dowd Home About Gallery Contact Privacy Notice My work in the community My work in Parliament My views Home About Gallery Contact Peter Dowd Labour Member of Parliament for Bootle Privacy Notice My work in the community My work in Parliament My views Contact Peter Address : Opportunities House 270 Marsh Lane, Bootle, L20 5BW Telephone : 0151 933 8432 Email : peter.dowd.mp@parliament.uk For any local issues, please contact your local councillors who are best placed to assist. Amendment 35 seeks to require drivers who are involved in a collision to not flee the scene and to report the collision to the police. Amendment 36 reduces the amount of time from 24 to 2 hours that a driver has to report a collision.
